text: Local industrial strategies and place-based development: Narratives of policy-making The cultural and creative industries (CCIs) have been highlighted as an important sector in the economy since the mapping the creative industries report was published in 1998. This has been continued through to the ‘creative industries sector deal’ and part of the UK’s Industrial Strategy. This chapter looks in detail to how narratives of CCI policymaking were included in the Local Industrial Strategy (LIS) pilots, carried out in two city-regions: the West Midlands and the North West of England. The analysis for this chapter uses discourse analysis which identified the social practices and rhetorical devices used to construct a discourse which articulated ‘the local’; that which constituted creativity; and the boundary work inherent in policy-making at the local level. The results uncovered a web of relationships between central and local government that is underpinned by a series of contractual obligations and agreements. Through the lens of Paasi’s regional geography of the borders of place, this study brings to light the discursive and social actions that occur within and between the borders of place. All whilst illustrating the level of agency that place-based actors are able to undertake through the use of policy-instruments that are enforced at the sub-national level to further devlop economic growth.
headline: Fully Human Written
